Home / Antiques
The Forest Chalet

Antiques

Alpine Style — Introduction

Alpine interiors are defined by natural materials, warm timber surfaces, and handcrafted architectural details. The aesthetic relies on authenticity, tactile textures, and the quiet presence of traditional craftsmanship. Within this environment, antique and vintage handmade objects play a structural role: they deepen the visual narrative, anchor the space culturally, and introduce the patina of time. The entire chalet interior is unified by a collection of antique hand-woven rugs, forming the artistic axis of the space and shaping its cultural rhythm.

Hand-Woven Rugs — Introduction

A collection of antique and vintage Persian and tribal rugs unifies every room of the chalet, forming the artistic axis of the interior.

No. 01
Kashan Rug
Persia · 326 × 241 cm
Classic Kashan medallion rug with symmetrical floral ornament and a brick-style border. Deep red field with cream, blue and green detailing. High quality wool, Persian knot (senneh). Iran, Kashan, ca. 1930–1950.
No. 02
Kashmar “Garden of Paradise”
Persia · 294 × 383 cm
Architectural and symbolic composition: arches, figures, garden motifs. Rich palette of deep blue, red, green and gold. Exceptional detail and high knot density typical of Kashmar workshops. Early–mid 20th century.
No. 03
Kashmar “Palace”
Persia · 298 × 378 cm
Palatial architectural rug with columns, arches, vessels and narrative elements. Strong Kashmar signature: complex layout, saturated colors (blue, red, cream, green), refined craftsmanship. Early–mid 20th century.
No. 04
Tabriz / Kashan Export Rug
Persia · 260 × 383 cm
Persian medallion rug with balanced floral–geometric ornament. Deep reds and blues contrasted with light medallion tones. High knot density, wool pile on cotton foundation. Produced ca. 1950–1960 for export to the U.S. market.
No. 05
Kurdish Nomadic Rug
Persia · 170 × 258 cm
Early 20th century nomadic rug with geometric medallions and natural dyes. Burgundy, crimson and dark green palette. Dense hand-knotted structure, visible age patina. Typical of Kurdish–Turkmen tribal weaving traditions.

More works from this collection will be added…

Porcelain, Bronze & Furnishings

Chinese imperial-style porcelain, ritual bronzes, and antique furnishings from India complete the collection, set against the chalet's timber interiors.

No. 06
Landscape Vase
China · Qianlong dynasty, per accompanying documentation
Porcelain vase painted with a landscape scene, displayed on a corner pedestal beneath a pair of botanical paintings.
No. 07
Yellow Vase with Peaches and Clouds
China · Tongzhi dynasty, per accompanying documentation
Yellow-ground porcelain vase decorated with peaches and clouds, shown beneath an original iris painting.
No. 08
Vase, “In Memory of Kangxi” Series
China · Yongzheng dynasty, per accompanying documentation
One of a pair of vases from the “In Memory of Kangxi” series, shown on a carved wooden stand beneath a lilac-themed painting.
No. 09
Vase, “In Memory of Kangxi” Series
China · Yongzheng dynasty, per accompanying documentation
The second vase of the pair from the “In Memory of Kangxi” series, displayed with a green porcelain vase beneath lilac and bellflower paintings.
No. 10
Large Imperial Vase with Birds and Flowers
China · Qianlong dynasty, per accompanying documentation
Large yellow-ground imperial vase with turquoise handles, painted with birds and flowering branches, on a wooden pedestal.
No. 11
Wine Vessel in the Form of a Rhinoceros
China · late 17th – early 18th century, per accompanying documentation
Bronze ritual wine vessel cast in the form of a rhinoceros with dense archaistic surface ornament, displayed on a carved wooden stand.
No. 12
Bell with a Buddhist Mantra
Bronze
Bronze bell inscribed with a Buddhist mantra, suspended on a tasselled cord beside the entrance hall.
No. 13
Coffee Table with Brass Cart Ornament
India · late 19th century
Coffee table incorporating a brass ornament from an antique cart, set on hand-woven rugs in the living room.
No. 14
Lid of an Antique Iron-Bound Chest
India · 19th century
Close-up of the riveted iron banding on the lid of an antique chest.
No. 15
Windowsill Group — Six Porcelain Pieces
China & Japan, per accompanying documentation
Left to right: 1) China, Yongzheng dynasty, monochrome “quail egg” glaze; 2) China, Yongzheng dynasty, matte monochrome peacock glaze; 3) China, Yongzheng dynasty, monochrome “pine needle” glaze; 4) Japan, Meiji period (1868–1912) painted porcelain in the Satsuma style, with an ivory-toned crackle glaze, multicolored overglaze enamels and gold decoration, typically depicting bijin (beautiful women) in kimono; 5) China, 1970s, figure of the God of Longevity; 6) China, Republic period, small vase with a sage.

More works from this collection will be added…

Sculpture, Mirrors & Further Porcelain

Carved teak figures from Burma, a hand-inlaid mirror from Peru, and further groupings of Qianlong-era porcelain and mixed-metal objects extend the collection through the chalet's living spaces.

No. 16
Budai (Laughing Buddha) with a Sack
Burma · mid-20th century · teak, per accompanying documentation
Carved teak figure of Budai, shown resting on an antique iron-bound chest.
No. 17
Sunburst Mirror
Peru · 1970s · wood with inlaid mirror-glass plates, handmade, per accompanying documentation
Hand-inlaid sunburst mirror in a carved wooden frame, shown beside a rosewood chest with copper-embossed panels (India, 1980, per accompanying documentation).
No. 18
Console Grouping — Five Porcelain Pieces
China · Qianlong, Daoguang & Guangxu dynasties, per accompanying documentation
1) Pink-ground vase with bats, Qianlong; 2) red double-gourd vase with pomegranates, Qianlong; 3) decorative plate, Guangxu; 4) vase with a grasshopper, Daoguang; 5) rectangular vase with irises, Yongzheng.
No. 19
Windowsill Grouping — Copper & Mixed-Metal Objects
Europe, per accompanying documentation
1) Copper kitchen utensils (mortar and bowl), 1960s, Europe; 2) nickel-silver candlestick, Bauhaus, handmade, 1930; 3) pouring jug decorated with cherries, 1860, per accompanying documentation; copper teapot with a wooden handle, Bauhaus, 1930, Germany; large copper kettle, Holland, 1890s. The painted tankard shown alongside is not yet catalogued.
No. 20
Three Monochrome-Glaze Vases
China · Qianlong dynasty, per accompanying documentation
1) Lavender monochrome vase; 2) small lavender vase with a poem and painted decoration; 3) monochrome vase in a “robin’s egg” glaze.
No. 21
Chandelier, Candelabra & Porcelain Vases
China, Italy & Spain, per accompanying documentation
1) Porcelain vase with court-life scenes, Qianlong; 2) porcelain vase with birds and trumpet-form spouts, Qianlong; crystal candelabra, Italy; crystal chandelier, Spain, 1990.

More works from this collection will be added…

Celadon & Monochrome-Glaze Porcelain

Further groupings of Chinese celadon and monochrome-glaze vases, together with Republic-period and export porcelain, continue the collection through the upper floor and hallway.

No. 22
Six Celadon & Monochrome Vases
China · Song & Qianlong dynasties, per accompanying documentation
Left to right: 1) matte blue-grey monochrome vase, double-gourd form, Yongzheng; 2) Ru ware vase, Song dynasty; 3–5) celadon imperial vases, Qianlong; 6) blue matte bottle-form vase, Yongzheng.
No. 23
Ge Ware Crackle-Glaze Vase
China · Southern Song dynasty, per accompanying documentation
Grey Ge ware vase with a pronounced large-scale crackle glaze, displayed on a corner pedestal.
No. 24
Porcelain Vases & Murano Perfume Bottles
China, Ming & Qing dynasties; Murano, Venice, per accompanying documentation
1) Yellow vase with green splashes, Hongzhi; 2) yellow vase with an engraved dragon and phoenix, Ming dynasty, Chenghua; 3) honey-glazed vase with a phoenix, Kangxi; 4) white vase with fish, Republic period, ca. 1920; together with a pair of Murano glass perfume bottles, Venice, 1940s.
No. 25
Three “Tea Dust” Glaze Vases
China · Yongzheng dynasty, per accompanying documentation
Baluster, cylinder and pear-shaped vases in a monochrome matte “tea dust” (cha-ye-mo) glaze.

More works from this collection will be added…

The Forest Chalet — Alpine Style

Across every room, the chalet's Alpine timber architecture continues to frame this growing collection — where antique porcelain, hand-woven rugs and carved wood furnishings sit quietly within the warmth of natural materials and handcrafted detail.
